slots.meaning: The protagonist's professional role/title.
slots.guidance: A single, unnamed civic-service role a newcomer is sworn into for one year; competent, morally serious, never a source of violence.

slots.meaning: The governance-shape epithet for the whole city (its constitutional structure in miniature).
slots.guidance: A short epithet naming how the city's power is structurally divided; wonder/gravity over dread; no lethal or toxic connotation.

slots.meaning: The proper name of the city the whole story takes place in.
slots.guidance: A single invented place name; the seat of every chamber; must read as governable and inhabited, never as a lethal or toxic environment.

slots.meaning: The constitutional pact binding the three chambers together.
slots.guidance: The foundational agreement that lets three rival bodies check one another without one seizing control; abstract, institutional, non-violent.

slots.meaning: The rare, closed joint meeting of all three chamber heads.
slots.guidance: A private session convened only when the chambers must face a shared problem together; procedural, tense, never physically dangerous.

slots.meaning: The periodic, decennial audit/judgment event that closes the story's year.
slots.guidance: A recurring, survivable institutional reckoning, not a physical threat; everyone lives to see the next one either way.

slots.meaning: The season whose civic hardship (a benefits cut) signals the deadline is approaching.
slots.guidance: A named season, not a sensory omen; its 'sign' is an administrative hardship (a cut ration), never physical danger.

slots.meaning: The institution that seconds/assigns the hero to a chamber for the year.
slots.guidance: A training or credentialing body named once at the outset; purely administrative.

slots.meaning: The named span of the hero's one-year posting.
slots.guidance: A fixed-term appointment name; must read as a bounded, survivable stretch of civic service.

slots.meaning: The specific quantity of the shortfall every chamber investigates from its own angle.
slots.guidance: A concrete counted amount of the story's resource; must read as a countable civic quantity, never a body count.

slots.meaning: The countable commodity whose shortfall drives the whole plot.
slots.guidance: A staple, storable, distributable good the city counts and rations; not a weapon, not a person.

slots.meaning: The population count the city keeps of itself.
slots.guidance: The record of who is officially counted as a resident; an administrative concept, not a person.

slots.meaning: The tax/collection system that gathers the resource from the counted population.
slots.guidance: The administrative mechanism that collects the resource against the census; abstract and procedural.

slots.meaning: The ancient founding register the city keeps of its own true count.
slots.guidance: A single old, authoritative document; the object of reverence and secrecy at the root of the mystery.

slots.meaning: The standing exception/loophole that has quietly covered the shortfall for years.
slots.guidance: A named, renewable legal exception; a bureaucratic euphemism for looking away, not a violent act.

slots.meaning: The great granary/vault at the center of the shortfall.
slots.guidance: The physical site where the resource is supposed to be stored; a place of counting and guarding, not danger.

slots.meaning: The city's district unit; also names the uncounted population living at the city's edge.
slots.guidance: A civic district word, singular or pluralized with a trailing 's' in context; never a place of captivity.

slots.meaning: The invented calendar's opening-of-year season, when the hero's posting begins.
slots.guidance: A season name marking the story's start; agricultural or cyclical in flavor, never ominous.

slots.meaning: The invented calendar's mid-year season.
slots.guidance: A season name marking the story's midpoint; distinct in feel from the other named seasons.

slots.meaning: The invented calendar's main, resource-gathering season.
slots.guidance: The season when the resource is brought in and the shortfall becomes undeniable; industrious, not threatening.

slots.meaning: The proper name of the first chamber, which judges and rules but never acts.
slots.guidance: A short institutional name for the deliberative/judicial chamber; grave, procedural.

slots.meaning: The bare office title of the first chamber's head.
slots.guidance: A single honorific word, used both alone and before the head's full name.

slots.meaning: The first chamber head's full personal name (no title).
slots.guidance: A first-and-last invented name; the same person as ROUTE_A_HEAD_SURNAME's surname.

slots.meaning: The first chamber head's surname alone, used in the vast majority of references.
slots.guidance: Must be the surname consistent with ROUTE_A_HEAD_NAME.

slots.meaning: The disgraced predecessor whose forced resignation closes one fault line in this chamber's story.
slots.guidance: A full invented name for a mid-level official; sympathetic, not villainous; never physically harmed.

slots.meaning: That predecessor's surname alone.
slots.guidance: Must be the surname consistent with ROUTE_A_CLERK.

slots.meaning: This chamber's generic term for a sealed judgment/ruling.
slots.guidance: The chamber's word for one of its own binding decisions; bureaucratic, not physically restraining.

slots.meaning: The proper name of the second chamber, which acts and provisions the city.
slots.guidance: A short institutional name for the executive/logistics chamber; busy, operational.

slots.meaning: The bare office title of the second chamber's head.
slots.guidance: A single honorific word, used both alone and before the head's full name.

slots.meaning: The second chamber head's full personal name (no title).
slots.guidance: A first-and-last invented name; the same person as ROUTE_B_HEAD_SURNAME's surname.

slots.meaning: The second chamber head's surname alone, used in the vast majority of references.
slots.guidance: Must be the surname consistent with ROUTE_B_HEAD_NAME.

slots.meaning: The proper name of the third chamber, which counts and remembers.
slots.guidance: A short institutional name for the record-keeping chamber; quiet, archival.

slots.meaning: The bare office title of the third chamber's head.
slots.guidance: A single honorific word, used both alone and before the head's full name.

slots.meaning: The third chamber head's full personal name (no title).
slots.guidance: A first-and-last invented name; the same person as ROUTE_C_HEAD_SURNAME's surname.

slots.meaning: The third chamber head's surname alone, used in the vast majority of references.
slots.guidance: Must be the surname consistent with ROUTE_C_HEAD_NAME.

slots.meaning: The junior clerk whose suppressed finding drives this chamber's central sub-plot.
slots.guidance: A full invented name for a junior official who was right too early; principled, never physically endangered.

slots.meaning: That clerk's surname alone.
slots.guidance: Must be the surname consistent with ROUTE_C_CLERK.

slots.meaning: The vault of sealed prior judgments the hero searches at night.
slots.guidance: A records room housing the chamber's own sealed history; atmospheric, not dangerous.

slots.meaning: The specific sealed instrument that has quietly authorized the shortfall for years.
slots.guidance: One particular old sealed ruling, distinct from the chamber's rulings in general.

slots.meaning: The physical seal on the archived instrument, renewed at each Reckoning.
slots.guidance: A small, tactile detail (a seal, stamp, or mark) the hero can read the age of.

slots.meaning: The ledger that claims a count for what the Deepstore's vault actually holds.
slots.guidance: The specific book the Deepstore-watch posting audits against the physical vault.

slots.meaning: The roster/log of guards posted on the Deepstore.
slots.guidance: A duty roster whose thickness the hero learns to read as a clue.

slots.meaning: The tax ledgers reconciling the levy against the census, ward by ward.
slots.guidance: The specific ledgers the levy-books posting audits.

slots.meaning: The ledger column that always reconciles even when the resource does not.
slots.guidance: A bookkeeping figure that can be made to balance regardless of the true resource flow.

slots.meaning: The transport used to move the resource on the provisioning run (used with a trailing 's' for the plural convoy).
slots.guidance: A single cart or transport unit; the convoy's basic unit.

slots.meaning: The shipping paperwork that is supposed to match every load (used with a trailing 's' for the plural).
slots.guidance: The paperwork the hero checks each transport against on the provisioning run.

slots.meaning: The worksite destination the provisioning run supplies.
slots.guidance: A place of labor the resource is delivered to; industrious, not dangerous.

slots.meaning: The junior clerk's suppressed written finding.
slots.guidance: A single short report documenting the recurring shortfall; a paper object, not a person.

slots.meaning: The clerk's full compiled backing evidence for the memo.
slots.guidance: A larger private file of supporting records kept by the clerk.

slots.meaning: The city's founding population/resource figure, inflated from the start.
slots.guidance: The oldest number in the Roll, the root of the recurring shortfall.
